rationale: Live-action UGC of a young man dancing has almost no overlap with Bounce Blast 3D's calm/satisfying physics-puzzle DNA — there's no character in the game, the moody dark-purple/amber palette clashes with the blue ambient bedroom lighting, and the hook references a different game ("Crossword Go"). Mechanically the dance hook shows zero gameplay, so it fails to communicate the tap-to-place + chain-reaction loop that drives this game's appeal. The 25–55 casual puzzle audience also skews older and more gameplay-curious than the Gen-Z TikTok dance demo this archetype attracts; only worth testing if reshot with an older talent doing a "satisfying chain-reaction reaction" overlay on actual gameplay.
